name: Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail
name_original: Asturias y Galicia — circular por el valle del río Navia
description: Tackle the difficult 22.7-mile (36.5 km) Asturias and Galicia circular mountain bike route through the scenic Navia river valley.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embalse de Salime offers diverse terrain characterized by the vast reservoir, surrounding mountains, and dense forests. The region features challenging ascents and descents through rugged landscapes, including the Sierras de Carondio and Valledor. Riders can expect a mix of forest paths, riverbanks, and elevated viewpoints overlooking the reservoir. This area provides a dynamic environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de Salime The most popular mountain bike trail is Interior Asturias GR 109 Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 12 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through varied mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail, a difficult 22.7 miles (36.5 km) path. This route navigates through a scenic gorge, offering a mix of technical sections and rewarding views. Local mountain bikers also love the Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de Salime is defined by dramatic reservoir views, rugged mountainous landscapes, and dense forests, with routes offering challenging terrain and significant elevation changes. The network provides options for experienced riders seeking demanding trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Salime's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Embalse de Salime?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Salime is diverse and challenging, characterized by the vast reservoir, surrounding mountains like the Sierras de Carondio and Valledor, and dense forests. You can expect a mix of forest paths, riverbanks, and elevated viewpoints, with significant ascents and descents. The routes often feature technical sections and flowy stretches, providing a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Embalse de Salime?
A: The mountain bike trails listed for Embalse de Salime are generally considered difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and technical sections. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485920/interior-asturias-gr-109-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interior Asturias GR 109 Loop&lt;/a&gt; involve over 2000 meters of elevation gain. While the region offers varied terrain, beginners or families with young children might find these specific routes quite demanding. It's advisable to check individual route details carefully for suitability.

Q: What are the best times of year to go mountain biking in Embalse de Salime?
A: The mountainous nature of Embalse de Salime means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow in higher areas, making some trails more challenging or inaccessible. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in the Embalse de Salime area?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes around Embalse de Salime. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210394950/bustelin-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482692/grandas-de-salime-pasando-por-el-rio-vavia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; are popular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location, offering convenient exploration of the region's landscapes.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain biking in Embalse de Salime offers dramatic reservoir views and rugged mountainous landscapes. You can expect to encounter dense forests, riverbanks, and elevated viewpoints such as the Mirador de A Paicega, which provides panoramic vistas of the reservoir and its surroundings. When water levels are low, you might even catch a glimpse of the submerged old village of Salime.

Q: What historical attractions are near the mountain bike trails in Embalse de Salime?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Embalse de Salime is rich in history. The Salime Dam itself is an architectural marvel with a notable 60-meter mural by Joaquín Vaquero and his son inside its turbine room. The area around Grandas de Salime is part of the Primitive Way of St. James, and you can explore the nearby Chao Samartín Hillfort and its museum for insights into ancient cultures.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in Embalse de Salime?
A: The mountain bike routes in Embalse de Salime vary in length and difficulty, influencing their duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485920/interior-asturias-gr-109-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interior Asturias GR 109 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at over 60 km with significant elevation, typically takes around 6 hours and 12 minutes to complete. Shorter routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482692/grandas-de-salime-pasando-por-el-rio-vavia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; (37.3 km), can be completed in about 3 hours and 44 minutes. Always consider your fitness level and planned stops.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for mountain biking in Embalse de Salime?
A: While general mountain biking is permitted on public trails, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas or private land. There are no specific permits mentioned for general mountain biking in the Embalse de Salime area. However, always stay on marked trails, respect wildlife, and follow the principles of Leave No Trace.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Embalse de Salime?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embalse de Salime are appreciated by the komoot community for their challenging terrain and dramatic scenery. Over 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied landscapes, often praising the demanding nature of the routes and the rewarding views of the reservoir and surrounding mountains.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Embalse de Salime?
A: There are at least 5 distinct mountain bike trails documented for the Embalse de Salime region, all of which are categorized as difficult due to their length and significant elevation changes. These routes offer a challenging and rewarding experience for experienced riders.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation near the mountain bike trails?
A: The Embalse de Salime region, particularly around towns like Grandas de Salime, offers amenities for visitors. While specific cafes directly on trails are rare, you can find places to eat and stay in the nearby villages. It's advisable to plan your route and check for services in advance, especially in more remote sections of the trails.
tips: Viewpoint of the Navia river towards the Salime reservoir and the Sierra de Asturias.
A viewpoint near the small mountain town of Arexo on the other side of the Navia river from the Galician side to Asturias.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the circular route through the Navia river valley?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike ride, classified as a "Hard mountain bike ride." It requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Expect technical sections, some of which may require dismounting and pushing your bike, aligning with approximately STS S3 - S6 on the Single Track Scale.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and often rugged. You'll encounter rocky trails, steep climbs, and varying surfaces. Given that Asturias and Galicia are part of "Green Spain," be prepared for potentially muddy sections, especially after rain. Good adherence is essential for the slopes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not suitable for beginners or families. It is a challenging trail designed for advanced riders with very good fitness and technical skills. Some sections are demanding enough to require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during the drier months to avoid excessive mud, though specific weather conditions can vary. Asturias and Galicia can experience wet weather, so always check the forecast before you go. Spring and early autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and more stable conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Navia river valley circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, you would typically look for parking in a nearby village or designated area that serves as a starting point for the circular route. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's route planner for the most convenient access points.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking on trails in this region does not require specific permits or entrance fees. However, always respect local regulations and private property. There are no indications from our sources that this particular route requires special permits.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking landscapes throughout the ride. The route traverses green valleys, offers spectacular views of the Navia River, and passes through lush countryside. You'll also see semi-abandoned villages in the mountains, providing glimpses into traditional life. Don't miss the View of the Navia River and the View of the Navia River from Arexo.

faqs.question: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming along the Navia River?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Navia River is known to have natural pools suitable for swimming. After a challenging ride, these spots can offer a refreshing break. It's a great way to cool down and enjoy the riverine beauty of the area.

faqs.question: Does this circular route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other trails. You will ride along parts of the Desfiladero de Bustelín (30% of the route), [CIMA LU05] Pedras Apañadas (4%), Pista da Ferraría da Cuíña (9%), and Ponte de Río de Porcos (2%).

faqs.question: Is the Asturias and Galicia - circular route through the Navia river valley dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor trails in Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations for this particular route are not detailed. Given the challenging nature of the terrain and the presence of potential wildlife or livestock in rural areas, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are fit enough for a difficult mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring essential mountain biking gear, including a well-maintained bike, helmet, appropriate protective clothing, and plenty of water and snacks. Due to the technical sections, consider bringing a repair kit and basic first aid. Weather can change, so layers are recommended.

waypoints.name: View of the Navia River from Arexo
